audit_items.proof_trace: do_item_get (items.c:666) increments it->refcount before returning the item pointer; every return path from do_add_delta calls do_item_remove(it) to balance this increment except the early-return at L3271-3273 when it->nbytes <= 2, which returns NON_NUMERIC with no corresponding do_item_remove call. This permanently inflates the item's refcount, preventing eviction and leaking the slab allocation.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker issues 'set key 0 0 0' (empty value, nbytes==2) then repeatedly sends 'incr key 1'; each incr call traverses the nbytes<=2 path in do_add_delta, incrementing the refcount by 1 without decrement, causing unbounded memory growth and eventual OOM (DoS).
